root_text.an5.164:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi …pe…
root_text.an5.164:1.2: pañcahi, āvuso, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁsājīvo sabrahmacārīnaṁ.
root_text.an5.164:1.4: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu attanā ca sīlasampanno hoti, sīlasampadākathāya ca āgataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.164:1.5: attanā ca samādhisampanno hoti, samādhisampadākathāya ca āgataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.164:1.6: attanā ca paññāsampanno hoti, paññāsampadākathāya ca āgataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.164:1.7: attanā ca vimuttisampanno hoti, vimuttisampadākathāya ca āgataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti;
root_text.an5.164:1.8: attanā ca vimuttiñāṇadassanasampanno hoti, vimuttiñāṇadassanasampadākathāya ca āgataṁ pañhaṁ byākattā hoti.
root_text.an5.164:1.9: Imehi kho, āvuso, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁsājīvo sabrahmacārīnan”ti.

translation_text.an5.164:1.1: There Venerable Sāriputta addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an5.164:1.2: “A mendicant with five qualities is fit to share their life with their spiritual companions.
translation_text.an5.164:1.4: A mendicant is personally accomplished in ethics, and answers questions that come up when discussing accomplishment in ethics.
translation_text.an5.164:1.8: They’re personally accomplished in the knowledge and vision of freedom, and they answer questions that come up when discussing accomplishment in the knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an5.164:1.9: A mendicant with these five qualities is fit to share their life with their spiritual companions.”
